name: Strict-Grammar i-Barrier
statement: Under the strict principal-branch EML grammar (eml(x,y) defined only when y > 0, terminal {1}), every well-defined tree evaluation is a real number. In particular, i = √(−1) is not constructible.

proof.line_2: x ∈ ℝ, y > 0 ⟹ eml(x,y) = exp(x) − ln(y) ∈ ℝ.
proof.line_3: Induction on depth: all values real. i ∉ ℝ. ∎

corollaries: No complex value is constructible under strict grammar.
Contrast: extended grammar's loophole activates at N=5 (Im = −π).
